risk_and_change_type.notes: CHANGE_TYPE=CONTENT is correct: all output files are under docs/golden-narrative/, no code changes, no SSOT modifications. RISK_LEVEL=STANDARD: per SOP §10 HIGH triggers, this sprint does not modify backend/core/analytics/, backend/core/contracts/, or control-plane paths. ADVISORY FLAG: The strategy document at lines 398-400 states 'Likely risk classification: HIGH, if architecture decisions touch compiler and contract authority.' This sprint's entire purpose is to make architecture decisions about the compiler and output contracts. The prompt author (GPT) declared STANDARD on the basis that file writes are docs-only. This is defensible under SOP §10 (no High-risk path modifications). However, if GPT intends the design authority document to carry governing weight over future HIGH-risk sprints, GPT should confirm STANDARD is the correct classification before Stage 3.
